frameworks.name: drift-diffusion
frameworks.one_line: Single-trial scalar combining deterministic drift and stochastic diffusion in LIP population activity.

frameworks.name: bounded evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: Population-derived integrator rises to a termination bound on individual decisions.

core_question: Can the trial-by-trial drift-diffusion signal underlying a perceptual decision be observed directly in simultaneously recorded LIP population activity?
key_assumptions: Perceptual decisions arise from temporal accumulation of noisy sensory evidence
Stochastic component of accumulation drives variability in choice and reaction time
A weighted sum of LIP neurons can recover a single decision variable
manipulations: Direction of random dot motion across trials
Simultaneous recording from hundreds of LIP neurons during decisions
key_findings: A weighted sum of LIP population activity yields a scalar combining deterministic drift and stochastic diffusion on single trials
This population-derived signal approximates the quantity governing variability in choice and reaction times
A small subset of neurons with response fields overlapping the choice targets carries the integral of noisy evidence
Direction-selective neurons with motion-stimulus response fields appear to represent the integrand
interpretations: Single-trial drift-diffusion is directly observable in LIP via a parsimonious two-subset architecture
State-space analyses without an explicit hypothesis would miss this low-dimensional integrator structure
limitations_noted: Signal requires targeting a small subset of neurons whose response fields overlap choice targets
